title: festuca drymeja subsp. exaltata
intro: Festuca drymeja subsp. exaltata, also known as mountain fescue, is a type of grass native to mountainous regions, particularly in the Mediterranean and parts of Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Festuca drymeja subsp. exaltata include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, while also promoting wound healing and reducing inflammation. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
